subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Shelter-in-Place Strategy
description: A short lesson to help you apply Shelter-in-Place Strategy.

hook.text: Tornado sirens sound. You have 13 minutes of average warning. Where do you go?

buildup.text: Interior rooms on the lowest floor—away from windows—provide the best protection from tornadoes.

discovery.text: Basements are ideal. Bathrooms and closets work—their small size and plumbing reinforce the structure.

twist.text: Mobile homes offer almost zero tornado protection even when tied down—always seek another shelter.

climax.text: Pre-identifying your shelter spot and practicing the route saves critical seconds during a real event.

punchline.text: Lowest floor. Interior room. No windows. Know where that is before the siren.

quiz.question: Where is the safest place during a tornado?
options: An interior room on the lowest floor away from windows
Near a large window to watch the storm
In a mobile home with tie-downs
